channel.title: Feeds Student Journalism and feeds about Journalism feeds

item.title: Woody Brown depicts diversity in communication through bestselling novel “Upward Bound”
item.description: Following the success of his debut novel, “Upward Bound,” Woody Brown returned to his alma mater to promote his book and discuss disability and communication. Brown graduated with a degree in English from UCLA in 2022 and went on to obtain a master’s degree from Columbia University.
item.source:markdown: Following the success of his debut novel, “Upward Bound,” Woody Brown returned to his alma mater to promote his book and discuss disability and communication. Brown graduated with a degree in English from UCLA in 2022 and went on to obtain a master’s degree from Columbia University.

item.title: UCLA softball axes Arkansas, eliminating Razorbacks from WCWS in run-rule shutout
item.description: This post was updated May 29 at 9:50 p.m. OKLAHOMA CITY — It is hard to stack runs in a single game.  It is even harder to plate an onslaught of runners in a single frame.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 29 at 9:50 p.m._ OKLAHOMA CITY — It is hard to stack runs in a single game. It is even harder to plate an onslaught of runners in a single frame.

item.title: Beat Breakdown: Which Bruin will succeed Chiles as UCLA gymnastics’s floor anchor?
item.description: When UCLA gymnastics’ season ended with a third-place finish at the national semifinal, it marked more than just the close of the Bruins’ 2026 campaign – it also brought an end to senior Jordan Chiles’ collegiate career.
item.source:markdown: When UCLA gymnastics’ season ended with a third-place finish at the national semifinal, it marked more than just the close of the Bruins’ 2026 campaign – it also brought an end to senior Jordan Chiles’ collegiate career.

item.title: UCPD arrests man suspected of sexual assault, cellphone theft
item.description: UCPD arrested a man early Friday morning after he allegedly assaulted multiple people on UCLA’s campus. The man allegedly stole a student’s cellphone on Bruin Walk and fled to the Hill where he attempted to sexually assault multiple students between 11:35 p.m.
item.source:markdown: UCPD arrested a man early Friday morning after he allegedly assaulted multiple people on UCLA’s campus. The man allegedly stole a student’s cellphone on Bruin Walk and fled to the Hill where he attempted to sexually assault multiple students between 11:35 p.m.

item.title: Saint Mary’s stuns UCLA baseball in LA regional opener
item.description: This post was updated May 29 at 4:50 p.m. It seemed like the Gaels came to Jackie Robinson Stadium looking for a boxing match Friday, with Mike Tyson stickers decorating their batting helmets.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 29 at 4:50 p.m._ It seemed like the Gaels came to Jackie Robinson Stadium looking for a boxing match Friday, with Mike Tyson stickers decorating their batting helmets.

item.title: Ocean preservationists dive in to the effects of DDT dumping at Hammer Museum event
item.description: Researchers across academic disciplines should work together to address the negative impacts of human interactions with and educate people about marine ecosystems, ocean preservation activists said at a May 21 event.
item.source:markdown: Researchers across academic disciplines should work together to address the negative impacts of human interactions with and educate people about marine ecosystems, ocean preservation activists said at a May 21 event.

item.title: What’s the latest on UCLA baseball’s pitching staff ahead of the NCAA LA Regional?
item.description: Coach John Savage said March 3 he didn’t think any team in the country would win over 50 games. No. 1 seed UCLA baseball is at 51.
item.source:markdown: Coach John Savage said March 3 he didn’t think any team in the country would win over 50 games. No. 1 seed UCLA baseball is at 51.

item.title: GSA condemns conversion of Weyburn Terrace to undergraduate housing
item.description: The Graduate Students Association criticized UCLA Housing’s decision to convert Weyburn Terrace into undergraduate housing, alleging a lack of input from graduate students. UCLA Housing is converting Weyburn Terrace into undergraduate housing over the next three years, it announced in a Jan.
item.source:markdown: The Graduate Students Association criticized UCLA Housing’s decision to convert Weyburn Terrace into undergraduate housing, alleging a lack of input from graduate students. UCLA Housing is converting Weyburn Terrace into undergraduate housing over the next three years, it announced in a Jan.

item.title: UCLA baseball NCAA tournament regional predictions 2026
item.description: Fresh off a Big Ten tournament championship title, No. 1 seed UCLA baseball (51-6, 28-2 Big Ten) will host its second-straight NCAA tournament regional as Virginia Tech (30-24, 15-15 ACC), Cal Poly (36-22, 22-8 Big West) and Saint Mary’s (34-25, 15-12 West Coast) travel to Westwood.
item.source:markdown: Fresh off a Big Ten tournament championship title, No. 1 seed UCLA baseball (51-6, 28-2 Big Ten) will host its second-straight NCAA tournament regional as Virginia Tech (30-24, 15-15 ACC), Cal Poly (36-22, 22-8 Big West) and Saint Mary’s (34-25, 15-12 West Coast) travel to Westwood.

item.title: UCLA baseball set to host 2nd consecutive regional
item.description: As the 15th seed of sixteen regional hosts, last years’ Bruin squad barely managed to secure home field advantage for the NCAA tournament. On the other hand, this year’s team had no reason to be concerned.
item.source:markdown: As the 15th seed of sixteen regional hosts, last years’ Bruin squad barely managed to secure home field advantage for the NCAA tournament. On the other hand, this year’s team had no reason to be concerned.

item.title: 12,000 employees to bring UC’s largest union to 60,000 workers
item.description: About 12,000 employees joined a union representing UC academic student employees, postdoctoral students and academic researchers May 19, making it the largest union in the UC.
item.source:markdown: About 12,000 employees joined a union representing UC academic student employees, postdoctoral students and academic researchers May 19, making it the largest union in the UC.

item.title: UCLA softball falls to Alabama in 1st round of Women’s College World Series
item.description: This post was updated May 28 at 7:20 p.m. OKLAHOMA CITY – The home run giveth, and the home run taketh away. The Bruins took the lead with back-to-back home runs and then lost the lead on Crimson Tide home runs in their OKC debut.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 28 at 7:20 p.m._ OKLAHOMA CITY – The home run giveth, and the home run taketh away. The Bruins took the lead with back-to-back home runs and then lost the lead on Crimson Tide home runs in their OKC debut.

item.title: Word from Walters: UCLA softball’s underclassmen are essential to the team
item.description: Megan Grant and Jordan Woolery dominate headlines. And for good reason, as the “Bruin Bombers” have racked up historic senior year campaigns, with Grant setting a single-season home run record with 40 and counting four-baggers, along with Woolery being on track to become the first player in NCAA history to boast a .500 batting average while hitting more than 30 home runs and plating more than 100 runners.
item.source:markdown: Megan Grant and Jordan Woolery dominate headlines. And for good reason, as the “Bruin Bombers” have racked up historic senior year campaigns, with Grant setting a single-season home run record with 40 and counting four-baggers, along with Woolery being on track to become the first player in NCAA history to boast a .500 batting average while hitting more than 30 home runs and plating more than 100 runners.

item.title: Opinion: Private security is not enough to make Hilgard safer for sororities
item.description: Private security is often a feature of high-profile events, with uniformed people who stiffly stand at the entrance checking bags and patting down patrons. The service is not often used for residential spaces.
item.source:markdown: Private security is often a feature of high-profile events, with uniformed people who stiffly stand at the entrance checking bags and patting down patrons. The service is not often used for residential spaces.

item.title: Word From Walters: Taylor Tinsley is UCLA softball’s key to postseason success
item.description: Jordan Chiles is That Girl. But Taylor Tinsley is UCLA softball’s rendition. The senior pitcher has pitched the sixth-most innings out of any pitcher in the nation, completing 215 frames while recording the second-most circle wins with 32 this season thus far.
item.source:markdown: Jordan Chiles is That Girl. But Taylor Tinsley is UCLA softball’s rendition. The senior pitcher has pitched the sixth-most innings out of any pitcher in the nation, completing 215 frames while recording the second-most circle wins with 32 this season thus far.

item.title: Think the media’s biased against you? You probably think misinformation is too
item.description: Ever feel like the news media is out to get you? That it skews its stories to make your side look bad? Okay — now what about the “fake news” media? All the misinformation out there online: Is it more unfair to your side of most arguments or the other one? Decades of communications research...
item.source:markdown: Ever feel like the news media is out to get you? That it skews its stories to make your side look bad? Okay — now what about the “fake news” media? All the misinformation out there online: Is it more unfair to your side of most arguments or the other one? Decades of communications research...

item.title: A battle of the Stars looms in D.C.’s shifting media scene
item.description: After The Washington Post laid off more than 300 journalists in February, several local and national news outlets based in the nation’s capital announced expansions to fill coverage gaps. Among newsrooms vying to step up where the Post was ceding ground, NOTUS emerged as the most ambitious. In March, it announced plans to double its...
item.source:markdown: After The Washington Post laid off more than 300 journalists in February, several local and national news outlets based in the nation’s capital announced expansions to fill coverage gaps. Among newsrooms vying to step up where the Post was ceding ground, NOTUS emerged as the most ambitious. In March, it announced plans to double its...

item.title: A look into the candidates running for California’s 36th congressional district
item.description: Democratic incumbent Ted Lieu and six challengers are running for California’s 36th congressional district, which encompasses UCLA and parts of West Los Angeles. Voters will choose the top two candidates for District 36 during the June 2 California primary election.
item.source:markdown: Democratic incumbent Ted Lieu and six challengers are running for California’s 36th congressional district, which encompasses UCLA and parts of West Los Angeles. Voters will choose the top two candidates for District 36 during the June 2 California primary election.

item.title: Micropayments for news have failed everywhere. Can they succeed in Kenya?
item.description: Reader revenue models are under strain worldwide. Audiences are overwhelmed by paywalls, trust in news is shrinking, and publishers are searching for new ways to persuade people to pay. In Kenya, some newsrooms are trying a different approach. Instead of asking for monthly commitments, they are testing whether readers will pay small amounts for a...
item.source:markdown: Reader revenue models are under strain worldwide. Audiences are overwhelmed by paywalls, trust in news is shrinking, and publishers are searching for new ways to persuade people to pay. In Kenya, some newsrooms are trying a different approach. Instead of asking for monthly commitments, they are testing whether readers will pay small amounts for a...

item.title: Daily Bruin Print Issue – May 28, 2026
item.description: The Daily Bruin publishes print issues every Monday, Wednesday and Friday during the school year. You can view the online version of The Bruin here. ﻿
item.source:markdown: _The Daily Bruin publishes print issues every Monday, Wednesday and Friday during the school year. You can view the online version of The Bruin here._ ﻿

item.title: UCLA softball Women’s College World Series predictions 2026
item.description: No. 8 seed UCLA softball (52-8, 20-4 Big Ten) will face No. 1 seed Alabama (54-7, 19-5 SEC) at Devon Park in Oklahoma City on Thursday afternoon to kickstart its Women’s College World Series campaign.
item.source:markdown: No. 8 seed UCLA softball (52-8, 20-4 Big Ten) will face No. 1 seed Alabama (54-7, 19-5 SEC) at Devon Park in Oklahoma City on Thursday afternoon to kickstart its Women’s College World Series campaign.

item.title: UCLA softball set to face Alabama in 1st round of Women’s College World Series
item.description: The greatest show on dirt returns. Eight teams are set for the final showdown of the 2026 softball campaign. No. 8 seed UCLA softball (52-8, 20-4 Big Ten) will face No.
item.source:markdown: The greatest show on dirt returns. Eight teams are set for the final showdown of the 2026 softball campaign. No. 8 seed UCLA softball (52-8, 20-4 Big Ten) will face No.

item.title: Restaurant review: Xibei Eatery serves flavorful northwestern Chinese cuisine in new Westwood spot
item.description: Westwood is benefiting from the addition of a new Chinese restaurant. Xibei Eatery, which opened a new location recently on Weyburn Avenue, specializes in northwestern Chinese cuisine.
item.source:markdown: Westwood is benefiting from the addition of a new Chinese restaurant. Xibei Eatery, which opened a new location recently on Weyburn Avenue, specializes in northwestern Chinese cuisine.

item.title: LA ballot measures could increase taxes on billionaires, hotels, sales
item.description: Four measures seeking to raise taxes will appear on Los Angeles’s June 2 ballot. The measures, if passed, would subsidize LA County’s public health system amid federal funding cuts and fill in tax gaps for unlicensed cannabis businesses and online travel companies.
item.source:markdown: Four measures seeking to raise taxes will appear on Los Angeles’s June 2 ballot. The measures, if passed, would subsidize LA County’s public health system amid federal funding cuts and fill in tax gaps for unlicensed cannabis businesses and online travel companies.

item.title: Gallery: Off to the WCWS! UCLA softball defeats UCF in LA Super Regional
item.description: No. 8 seed UCLA softball (52-8, 20-4 Big Ten) hosted UCF (41-19-1, 14-9-1 Big 12) on Friday and Saturday for the NCAA Los Angeles Super Regional at Easton Stadium.
item.source:markdown: No. 8 seed UCLA softball (52-8, 20-4 Big Ten) hosted UCF (41-19-1, 14-9-1 Big 12) on Friday and Saturday for the NCAA Los Angeles Super Regional at Easton Stadium.

item.title: California Senate passes UC-sponsored bill proposing $12B research bond
item.description: This post was updated May 28 at 12:03 a.m. The California Senate voted Wednesday in favor of a bill that would put a $12 billion bond for scientific research on the November 2026 ballot.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 28 at 12:03 a.m._ The California Senate voted Wednesday in favor of a bill that would put a $12 billion bond for scientific research on the November 2026 ballot.

item.title: Opinion: Cooking classes pose a blooming benefit to UCLA, student success
item.description: UCLA prides itself on student wellness. But for a campus filled with pre-medical and health-focused students, it fails to teach one of the most basic life skills: cooking.
item.source:markdown: UCLA prides itself on student wellness. But for a campus filled with pre-medical and health-focused students, it fails to teach one of the most basic life skills: cooking.

item.title: Hundreds of UC faculty call to reinstate SAT, ACT requirements for STEM applicants
item.description: This post was updated May 28 at 12:04 p.m. More than 600 UC faculty are calling on the University to reinstate standardized testing requirements for undergraduate STEM applicants, alleging that students are not prepared enough for college-level coursework.  The faculty signed on to a letter asking the UC Board of Regents, the UC Office of the President and Academic Senate leadership to require undergraduate STEM applicants to submit an SAT or ACT math score, beginning with the 2027-28 admissions cycle.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 28 at 12:04 p.m._ More than 600 UC faculty are calling on the University to reinstate standardized testing requirements for undergraduate STEM applicants, alleging that students are not prepared enough for college-level coursework. The faculty signed on to a letter asking the UC Board of Regents, the UC Office of the President and Academic Senate leadership to require undergraduate STEM applicants to submit an SAT or ACT math score, beginning with the 2027-28 admissions cycle.

item.title: Editorial Cartoon: “Hidden Fees”
Opinion: Going to UCLA is stressful. Going to the gym shouldn’t be.
item.description: Going to the gym at UCLA is more of a mental workout than a physical one. It’s supposed to be simple: students go to the gym to clear their heads between classes or so they don’t run out of breath trudging up Bruin Walk.
item.source:markdown: Going to the gym at UCLA is more of a mental workout than a physical one. It’s supposed to be simple: students go to the gym to clear their heads between classes or so they don’t run out of breath trudging up Bruin Walk.

item.title: The emerging AI content licensing market puts news publishers in a “double bind,” a new report warns
item.description: A new report from the thinktank Open Markets Institute scopes out the current state of AI content licensing for news publishers. “Same Gatekeepers, New Tollbooths: Mapping the AI Content Licensing Market” explores the emerging market for content licensing, arguing that news publishers are currently in a “double bind”: The same big tech companies that are...
item.source:markdown: A new report from the thinktank Open Markets Institute scopes out the current state of AI content licensing for news publishers. “Same Gatekeepers, New Tollbooths: Mapping the AI Content Licensing Market” explores the emerging market for content licensing, arguing that news publishers are currently in a “double bind”: The same big tech companies that are...

item.title: UCLA men’s golf places 2nd in NCAA regional, secures spot in championship
item.description: This post was updated May 27 at 11:24 p.m. The road back to the national championship is not always easy. But the Bruins are headed back.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 27 at 11:24 p.m._ The road back to the national championship is not always easy. But the Bruins are headed back.

item.title: The Economist launches a dedicated ChatGPT app
item.description: On Wednesday, The Economist launched its own ChatGPT app — the first of its kind by a major consumer news publication. “The Economist – Graphs” runs natively inside ChatGPT and allows users to interact with the publication’s data visualizations. At launch, the app is focused solely on U.S. polling data. After installing the app, ChatGPT...
item.source:markdown: On Wednesday, The Economist launched its own ChatGPT app — the first of its kind by a major consumer news publication. “The Economist – Graphs” runs natively inside ChatGPT and allows users to interact with the publication’s data visualizations. At launch, the app is focused solely on U.S. polling data. After installing the app, ChatGPT...

In a time of growing censorship, creators must fight for authenticity.

item.description:
Director Boots Riley dresses up his newest social satire under layers of humor.

USC left Omaha with a win but continued to stumble against UCLA.

item.description:
The women placed third while the men placed fourth, winning a total of nine titles.

The women placed third while the men placed fourth, winning a total of nine titles.

item.description:
Rejecting binary labels regarding mental health is pivotal for more nuanced care.

item.title: UCLA track and field to continue season in Arkansas, approach end of campaign
item.description: This post was updated May 27 at 11:22 p.m. Track and field athletes are no stranger to long, strenuous seasons. But the Bruins will enter the homestretch of their season with postseason aspirations on the horizon.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 27 at 11:22 p.m._ Track and field athletes are no stranger to long, strenuous seasons. But the Bruins will enter the homestretch of their season with postseason aspirations on the horizon.

item.title: In Plain Sight: From murals to evacuation maps, UCLA’s sign shop blends craftsmanship, creativity
item.description: A diverse range of signs are scattered across UCLA’s 419-acre campus, from murals to traffic markers. But behind the scenes, workers at UCLA’s sign shop spend weeks in the basement of the Facilities Management building creating the signs that guide students, faculty and visitors.
item.source:markdown: A diverse range of signs are scattered across UCLA’s 419-acre campus, from murals to traffic markers. But behind the scenes, workers at UCLA’s sign shop spend weeks in the basement of the Facilities Management building creating the signs that guide students, faculty and visitors.

item.title: DOJ launches second suit against UC, alleging antisemitism against UCLA students
item.description: This post was updated May 27 at 11:10 p.m. The United States Department of Justice is again suing the UC over alleged antisemitism, claiming that UCLA allowed discrimination against Jewish and Israeli students.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 27 at 11:10 p.m._ The United States Department of Justice is again suing the UC over alleged antisemitism, claiming that UCLA allowed discrimination against Jewish and Israeli students.

item.title: UCLA to combine administrative roles, use settlement funds for Athletics deficit
item.description: This post was updated May 27 at 11:45 p.m. UCLA will use bond funds to reduce UCLA Athletics department’s budget deficit and consolidate its chief financial officer and administrative vice chancellor roles, Chancellor Julio Frenk announced in a Tuesday morning State of the Campus address.  Frenk outlined a three-step plan to address UCLA’s financial shortfalls at the inaugural address, which university administrators, student government leaders, faculty and UC Office of the President representatives attended.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 27 at 11:45 p.m._ UCLA will use bond funds to reduce UCLA Athletics department’s budget deficit and consolidate its chief financial officer and administrative vice chancellor roles, Chancellor Julio Frenk announced in a Tuesday morning State of the Campus address. Frenk outlined a three-step plan to address UCLA’s financial shortfalls at the inaugural address, which university administrators, student government leaders, faculty and UC Office of the President representatives attended.

item.title: FAST runway show threads together couture, ambition and self-expression
item.description: This post was updated May 27 at 9:20 p.m. Crochet, corsets and camp took center stage Friday night. Fashion and Student Trends at UCLA held its 25th annual spring runway show at Pauley Pavilion on Friday at 7 p.m.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 27 at 9:20 p.m._ Crochet, corsets and camp took center stage Friday night. Fashion and Student Trends at UCLA held its 25th annual spring runway show at Pauley Pavilion on Friday at 7 p.m.

item.title: Hanlin Su’s story-driven animation brings Alan Walker’s song ‘Faded’ to life
item.description: This post was updated May 27 at 8:57 p.m. Hanlin Su has been telling stories since before he knew what he was doing. Su, a first-year psychology student, runs Retrever Entertainment, a boutique animation studio he launched after enrolling at UCLA last fall.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 27 at 8:57 p.m._ Hanlin Su has been telling stories since before he knew what he was doing. Su, a first-year psychology student, runs Retrever Entertainment, a boutique animation studio he launched after enrolling at UCLA last fall.

item.title: Low-Entry Online Casinos and the Shift Toward More Controlled Digital Spending
item.description: Low-entry casinos are a new player in the gambling world, and unsurprisingly, they are changing how players spend online. What’s interesting is that low-entry sites mirror broader digital habits (e.g., in e-commerce and gaming) where smaller purchases feel easier and more controlled than large upfront financial commitments.
item.source:markdown: Low-entry casinos are a new player in the gambling world, and unsurprisingly, they are changing how players spend online. What’s interesting is that low-entry sites mirror broader digital habits (e.g., in e-commerce and gaming) where smaller purchases feel easier and more controlled than large upfront financial commitments.

item.title: Moving Out of the Dorms? Here’s What To Do With Everything That Won’t Fit Back Home
item.description: Lease agreements are ending, university housing doors lock in exactly four days and your flight home is already booked. Now you’re looking around your room at a bed frame, a defrosting mini-fridge and a year’s worth of stuff that clearly won’t fit into a suitcase.
item.source:markdown: **Lease agreements are ending, university housing doors lock in exactly four days and your flight home is already booked. Now you’re looking around your room at a bed frame, a defrosting mini-fridge and a year’s worth of stuff that clearly won’t fit into a suitcase.**

item.title: HAND at UCLA brings researchers, students together to discuss aging and memory
item.description: Subtle memory-related brain changes in people with neurological disorders may be reversible before full cognitive decline occurs, underscoring the importance of early detection, UCLA researchers said at a May 15 conference.
item.source:markdown: Subtle memory-related brain changes in people with neurological disorders may be reversible before full cognitive decline occurs, underscoring the importance of early detection, UCLA researchers said at a May 15 conference.

item.title: Administrative vice chancellor Michael Beck to retire at the end of 2026
item.description: This post was updated May 26 at 7:05 p.m. UCLA’s administrative vice chancellor will retire at the end of 2026, he announced Tuesday. Michael Beck, who stepped into the role in 2016, oversees UCLA Housing and Hospitality, facilities management, transportation, procurement and information technology.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 26 at 7:05 p.m._ UCLA’s administrative vice chancellor will retire at the end of 2026, he announced Tuesday. Michael Beck, who stepped into the role in 2016, oversees UCLA Housing and Hospitality, facilities management, transportation, procurement and information technology.

item.title: Scouting report: UCLA softball vs. Alabama
item.description: No. 8 seed UCLA softball (52-8, 20-4 Big Ten) will face No. 1 seed Alabama (54-7, 19-5 SEC) on Thursday afternoon in the first round of the Women’s College World Series.
item.source:markdown: No. 8 seed UCLA softball (52-8, 20-4 Big Ten) will face No. 1 seed Alabama (54-7, 19-5 SEC) on Thursday afternoon in the first round of the Women’s College World Series.

item.title: Opinion: College may breed competition, but community building should be at the forefront
item.description: Millions of high school seniors in the United States are meticulously planning their next steps. With an excess of online discourse about choosing a school, college is seen as the biggest contract a young adult can sign.
item.source:markdown: Millions of high school seniors in the United States are meticulously planning their next steps. With an excess of online discourse about choosing a school, college is seen as the biggest contract a young adult can sign.

item.title: Entertainment Unscripted: Marialena Lampirianidis
item.description: Listen to Marialena Lampirianidis share her story from just starting out her career in entertainment after UCLA to working at Zero Gravity Management and Innovative Artists.
item.source:markdown: Listen to Marialena Lampirianidis share her story from just starting out her career in entertainment after UCLA to working at Zero Gravity Management and Innovative Artists.

item.title: Erin Brockovich made a map to track data centers around the country
item.description: Erin Brockovich, the environmental activist whose name and work you may recognize from the Oscar-winning movie Erin Brockovich, has created a tool to map data centers across the country, along with a form for people to report data centers and their impacts in their community. “The RACE to build AI infrastructures is unfolding town by...
item.source:markdown: Erin Brockovich, the environmental activist whose name and work you may recognize from the Oscar-winning movie Erin Brockovich, has created a tool to map data centers across the country, along with a form for people to report data centers and their impacts in their community. “The RACE to build AI infrastructures is unfolding town by...

item.title: You couldn’t create a more anti-news internet if you tried
item.description: If there were a dictator of the internet who intentionally set out to destroy your ability to get accurate information, the result would look a lot like what’s already on your screen. But why? I mentioned here a couple of weeks ago that I’ve been studying economics to find more rigorous frameworks to describe why...
item.source:markdown: If there were a dictator of the internet who intentionally set out to destroy your ability to get accurate information, the result would look a lot like what’s already on your screen. But why? I mentioned here a couple of weeks ago that I’ve been studying economics to find more rigorous frameworks to describe why...

item.title: Daily Bruin Print Issue – May 26, 2026
item.description: You can view the online version of The Bruin here.
item.source:markdown: You can view the online version of The Bruin here.

item.title: Professors express concern following Trump administration’s proposed funding cuts
item.description: The Trump administration requested more than $15 billion in cuts to federal research funding for a second consecutive year, leaving UCLA professors concerned about the future of scientific research.
item.source:markdown: The Trump administration requested more than $15 billion in cuts to federal research funding for a second consecutive year, leaving UCLA professors concerned about the future of scientific research.

item.title: IT employees across UC vote to join UPTE-CWA 9119 union
item.description: More than 2,000 information technology and technical employees across the UC unionized Thursday. The employees voted to unionize with University Professional and Technical Employees-Communications Workers of America 9119 – which now represents about 8,400 workers in its technology bargaining unit – forming the largest union of its kind in the United States, a union spokesperson said in an emailed statement.
item.source:markdown: More than 2,000 information technology and technical employees across the UC unionized Thursday. The employees voted to unionize with University Professional and Technical Employees-Communications Workers of America 9119 – which now represents about 8,400 workers in its technology bargaining unit – forming the largest union of its kind in the United States, a union spokesperson said in an emailed statement.

item.title: Speciality hand-pulled noodle restaurant opens fourth LA area location in Westwood
item.description: Xibei Eatery, a Chinese hand-pulled noodle chain, opened in Westwood this spring. With more than 380 locations across China, Xibei Eatery’s Westwood location is the fourth to open in the Los Angeles area.
item.source:markdown: Xibei Eatery, a Chinese hand-pulled noodle chain, opened in Westwood this spring. With more than 380 locations across China, Xibei Eatery’s Westwood location is the fourth to open in the Los Angeles area.

item.title: UCLA faculty, students voice concern over discontinued media literacy class
item.description: UCLA’s Teacher Education Program plans to discontinue one of its signature course offerings, faculty said. The program’s critical media literacy classes help students analyze media, evaluate the quality of information sources and examine representation online, according to the TEP website.
item.source:markdown: UCLA’s Teacher Education Program plans to discontinue one of its signature course offerings, faculty said. The program’s critical media literacy classes help students analyze media, evaluate the quality of information sources and examine representation online, according to the TEP website.

item.title: Gallery: Chinese American Culture Night brings dance, music to Royce Hall
item.description: The 37th Annual Chinese American Culture Night was held at Royce Hall on May 2. This year’s play, “The Parts We Played,” followed a Chinese American theater troupe and its journey gaining representation on stage in the 1970s.
item.source:markdown: The 37th Annual Chinese American Culture Night was held at Royce Hall on May 2. This year’s play, “The Parts We Played,” followed a Chinese American theater troupe and its journey gaining representation on stage in the 1970s.

item.title: USAC recap – April 21
item.description: The Undergraduate Students Association Council allocated more than $150,000 in excess funding to student organizations at its April 21 meeting. USAC is the official student government representing the undergraduate student body at UCLA.
item.source:markdown: The Undergraduate Students Association Council allocated more than $150,000 in excess funding to student organizations at its April 21 meeting. USAC is the official student government representing the undergraduate student body at UCLA.

item.title: Film review: ‘The Mandalorian and Grogu’ chokes on its aspirations with lack of novelty
item.description: Warning: Spoilers ahead. Sputtering into theaters by jetpack Friday, “Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu” concluded Disney’s beloved “Mandalorian” franchise with an engine failure of a narrative.
item.source:markdown: Warning: Spoilers ahead. Sputtering into theaters by jetpack Friday, “Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu” concluded Disney’s beloved “Mandalorian” franchise with an engine failure of a narrative.

item.title: Third walk-off wins UCLA baseball Big Ten championship against Oregon in extras
item.description: This post was updated May 25 at 9:52 p.m. The comeback kids did it again.  No. 1 seed UCLA (51-6, 28-2 Big Ten) bested No. 3 seed Oregon (40-16, 20-10) 3-2 in 11 innings to win the Big Ten championship, achieving its third walk-off victory of the conference tournament in just as many days.  With the bases loaded and the score tied 2-2 in the bottom of the 11th, Oregon right-hander Devin Bell sailed a fastball high and tight to junior infielder Phoenix Call, which the home plate umpire ruled as a hit-by-pitch.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 25 at 9:52 p.m._ The comeback kids did it again. No. 1 seed UCLA (51-6, 28-2 Big Ten) bested No. 3 seed Oregon (40-16, 20-10) 3-2 in 11 innings to win the Big Ten championship, achieving its third walk-off victory of the conference tournament in just as many days. With the bases loaded and the score tied 2-2 in the bottom of the 11th, Oregon right-hander Devin Bell sailed a fastball high and tight to junior infielder Phoenix Call, which the home plate umpire ruled as a hit-by-pitch.

item.title: Opinion: Care for women’s sports is just some curiosity and a Google search away
item.description: April 5, 2026, was the proudest I have ever felt as a Bruin. Our women’s basketball team beat the South Carolina Gamecocks 79-51 in the NCAA championship.
item.source:markdown: April 5, 2026, was the proudest I have ever felt as a Bruin. Our women’s basketball team beat the South Carolina Gamecocks 79-51 in the NCAA championship.

item.title: Opinion: Bruin Walk’s aggressive advertising culture pushes students away, needs reform
item.description: A swarm of bodies is blocking your way, begging for attention. They keep talking to you, despite your disinterest. They even ask for your autograph. You could be an A-list celebrity – or a UCLA student walking down Bruin Walk.
item.source:markdown: A swarm of bodies is blocking your way, begging for attention. They keep talking to you, despite your disinterest. They even ask for your autograph. You could be an A-list celebrity – or a UCLA student walking down Bruin Walk.

item.title: Interim dean of Luskin School of Public Affairs soon to be permanently instated
item.description: This post was updated May 25 at 11:00 p.m. A distinguished professor in urban planning will become the permanent dean of the UCLA Luskin School of Public Affairs on June 1.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 25 at 11:00 p.m._ A distinguished professor in urban planning will become the permanent dean of the UCLA Luskin School of Public Affairs on June 1.

item.title: Winner takes all: The best, this ‘Star Wars’ movie is. After nearly 50 years, which film is on top?
item.description: This post was updated May 25 at 8:18 p.m. “Star Wars” is known for its galaxywide fame and success. Since its debut in 1977, the “Star Wars” franchise has expanded far beyond a single trilogy, growing into one of the most influential sci-fi sagas in cinematic history.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 25 at 8:18 p.m._ “Star Wars” is known for its galaxywide fame and success. Since its debut in 1977, the “Star Wars” franchise has expanded far beyond a single trilogy, growing into one of the most influential sci-fi sagas in cinematic history.

item.title: UCLA softball scores 10-run win over UCF, advances to Women’s College World Series
item.description: This post was updated May 25 at 10:11 p.m. No one has more home runs – with 200 – in a single season.  Now, no one has more runs in a single season – with 651 – than the Oklahoma City-bound Bruins.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 25 at 10:11 p.m._ No one has more home runs – with 200 – in a single season. Now, no one has more runs in a single season – with 651 – than the Oklahoma City-bound Bruins.

item.title: 2 days, 2 walk-offs: Mulivai Levu pushes UCLA baseball past USC in 7-5 victory
item.description: This post was updated May 23 at 5:45 p.m. On back-to-back days at the Big Ten tournament, junior first baseman Mulivai Levu was the walk-off hero.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 23 at 5:45 p.m._ On back-to-back days at the Big Ten tournament, junior first baseman Mulivai Levu was the walk-off hero.

item.title: Gallery: Following 2026 Winter Olympics, Stars on Tour features world champion skaters
item.description: World-class skaters took to the ice at the Honda Center in Anaheim as part of the Stars on Ice Tour on Saturday night. The event featured group and solo performances by Olympic and world champion skaters, including Alysa Liu and Amber Glen.
item.source:markdown: World-class skaters took to the ice at the Honda Center in Anaheim as part of the Stars on Ice Tour on Saturday night. The event featured group and solo performances by Olympic and world champion skaters, including Alysa Liu and Amber Glen.

item.title: Concert review: Student Committee for the Arts hosts next dream-pop icon at annual Royce Hall show
item.description: This post was updated May 25 at 7:50 p.m. Before, many attendees may not have known exactly who they were watching this evening. After, it would be hard to forget.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 25 at 7:50 p.m._ Before, many attendees may not have known exactly who they were watching this evening. After, it would be hard to forget.

item.title: UCLA baseball defeats Purdue in walk-off fashion, advances to Big Ten semifinal
item.source:markdown: Nine hours and 20 minutes after the originally posted game time, junior first baseman Mulivai Levu put a bow on the never-ending day. The Bruins had to wait an eternity for a celebration, but it was well worth it. No.

item.title: UCLA softball secures run-rule victory over UCF in 1st game of NCAA Super Regional
item.description: The story of the Bruins’ season has been home runs and high-scoring games. But with a 3-1 lead entering the bottom of the fifth inning, this game looked to be an exception.
item.source:markdown: The story of the Bruins’ season has been home runs and high-scoring games. But with a 3-1 lead entering the bottom of the fifth inning, this game looked to be an exception.

item.title: 5 candidates are running in primary to represent California Assembly District 51
item.description: Two Democrats – including an incumbent – and a Republican retired businessman are among the candidates vying to represent UCLA and its surrounding areas in the state assembly.
item.source:markdown: Two Democrats – including an incumbent – and a Republican retired businessman are among the candidates vying to represent UCLA and its surrounding areas in the state assembly.

item.title: UCLA historians preserve documents, photos in ‘Power from the Past’ online archive
item.description: UCLA historians launched a revamped digital labor history archive in April, preserving materials from movements led by Southern California unions. The Memory Work Research Initiative at UCLA – which the Institute for Research on Labor and Employment oversees – digitized documents, images and flyers on an online archive called Power from the Past.
item.source:markdown: UCLA historians launched a revamped digital labor history archive in April, preserving materials from movements led by Southern California unions. The Memory Work Research Initiative at UCLA – which the Institute for Research on Labor and Employment oversees – digitized documents, images and flyers on an online archive called Power from the Past.

item.title: How did sports betting become so normalized among college students?
item.description: Americans were estimated to have legally bet more than $3.3 billion on the NCAA March Madness tournament this year. Sports betting has gained traction among young people in recent years.
item.source:markdown: Americans were estimated to have legally bet more than $3.3 billion on the NCAA March Madness tournament this year. Sports betting has gained traction among young people in recent years.

item.title: UCLA softball Los Angeles Super Regional predictions
item.description: No. 8 seed UCLA softball (50-8, 20-4 Big Ten) will face UCF (41-17-1, 14-9-1 Big 12) in the Los Angeles Super Regional from Friday to Sunday.
item.source:markdown: No. 8 seed UCLA softball (50-8, 20-4 Big Ten) will face UCF (41-17-1, 14-9-1 Big 12) in the Los Angeles Super Regional from Friday to Sunday.

item.title: UCLA softball prepares for weekend Los Angeles super regional
item.source:markdown:
“War of attrition, war of wills.”
Columnist George Vecsey’s infamous quote describes the postseason like no other – after a season of triumphs and disappointments, all is for naught without composure, character and rising up to the challenge on the largest stage.

item.title: UCLA baseball Big Ten tournament predictions 2026
item.description: No. 1 seed UCLA baseball (48-6, 28-2 Big Ten) has officially concluded its regular season and will head to the Big Ten tournament in Omaha after receiving a bye to the single-elimination stage.
item.source:markdown: No. 1 seed UCLA baseball (48-6, 28-2 Big Ten) has officially concluded its regular season and will head to the Big Ten tournament in Omaha after receiving a bye to the single-elimination stage.

item.title: Five things: What could go wrong for UCLA baseball? Well, here are 5 options
item.description: No. 1 UCLA baseball (48-6, 28-2 Big Ten) became the first team to ever spend every week of the regular season at the very top of the top 25.
item.source:markdown: No. 1 UCLA baseball (48-6, 28-2 Big Ten) became the first team to ever spend every week of the regular season at the very top of the top 25.

item.title: AFSCME Local 3299 employees vote to ratify contract
item.description: This post was updated May 22 at 12:29 p.m. A union that represents more than 40,000 workers – and that has been in negotiations with the UC for more than two years – voted to ratify a contract with the University on Friday.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 22 at 12:29 p.m._ A union that represents more than 40,000 workers – and that has been in negotiations with the UC for more than two years – voted to ratify a contract with the University on Friday.

item.title: In Plain Sight: UCLA Marina Aquatic Center staff provides space for recreation, water sports
item.description: This post was updated May 22 at 1:54 a.m. About 10 miles away from campus, staff at UCLA’s waterfront recreation center are providing a natural escape for students and veterans.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 22 at 1:54 a.m._ About 10 miles away from campus, staff at UCLA’s waterfront recreation center are providing a natural escape for students and veterans.

item.title: Professor Catherine Sugar will be 2027-28 chair of Academic Senate
item.description: A professor with appointments in three UCLA departments will serve as the 2027-28 chair of the Academic Senate. Catherine Sugar, who will begin her term as chair Sept.
item.source:markdown: A professor with appointments in three UCLA departments will serve as the 2027-28 chair of the Academic Senate. Catherine Sugar, who will begin her term as chair Sept.

item.title: Daily Bruin Print Issue – May 22, 2026
item.description: The Daily Bruin publishes print issues every Monday, Wednesday and Friday during the school year. You can view the online version of The Bruin here. ﻿
item.source:markdown: The Daily Bruin publishes print issues every Monday, Wednesday and Friday during the school year. You can view the online version of The Bruin here. ﻿

item.title: This costume designer dressed ‘Dune.’ She’s teaching UCLA students her craft.
item.description: Jacqueline West drapes a new vision for world-building in a classroom where students stitch sandstorms from silk. The Academy Award nominee is the latest David C.
item.source:markdown: Jacqueline West drapes a new vision for world-building in a classroom where students stitch sandstorms from silk. The Academy Award nominee is the latest David C.

item.title: Opinion: Police on campus surveil, not support, students
item.description: This post was updated May 21 at 8:46 p.m. UCLA students are no strangers to surveillance. One stroll down Bruin Walk is all the proof you need.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated May 21 at 8:46 p.m._ UCLA students are no strangers to surveillance. One stroll down Bruin Walk is all the proof you need.

item.title: As goes CBS Radio News, so goes the idea that news media should serve the public interest
item.description: When CBS Radio News goes silent on May 22, 2026, Americans will lose access to news programming they’ve tuned into from their living rooms, kitchens and cars for nearly a century. The once-bipartisan idea that the nation’s media should exist to serve democracy continues to fade with it, too. As a media historian, I think...
item.source:markdown: When CBS Radio News goes silent on May 22, 2026, Americans will lose access to news programming they’ve tuned into from their living rooms, kitchens and cars for nearly a century. The once-bipartisan idea that the nation’s media should exist to serve democracy continues to fade with it, too. As a media historian, I think...

item.title: More than 340 local news outlets are limiting the Internet Archive’s access to their journalism
item.description: In January, Nieman Lab broke the story that major news publishers — including The New York Times, The Guardian, and USA Today Co. — had started blocking the Internet Archive due to concerns that AI companies might scrape the nonprofit’s repositories for training data. No news publisher has confirmed to Nieman Lab that an AI...
item.source:markdown: In January, Nieman Lab broke the story that major news publishers — including The New York Times, The Guardian, and USA Today Co. — had started blocking the Internet Archive due to concerns that AI companies might scrape the nonprofit’s repositories for training data. No news publisher has confirmed to Nieman Lab that an AI...

item.title: James Murdoch buys up half of Vox Media, grabbing New York and podcasts, but leaving The Verge and SB Nation
item.description: This is May 2026 in digital media: Arguably the two most prominent digital media startups of the 2010s are both being sold — one to the former host of NBC reality show “Real People” (1979-84) and one to the primary inspiration for Kendall Roy (2018-23). On May 11, it was standup-comic-turned-media-mogul Byron Allen acquiring a...
item.source:markdown: This is May 2026 in digital media: Arguably the two most prominent digital media startups of the 2010s are both being sold — one to the former host of NBC reality show “Real People” (1979-84) and one to the primary inspiration for Kendall Roy (2018-23). On May 11, it was standup-comic-turned-media-mogul Byron Allen acquiring a...

item.title: Tech journalist Joanna Stern on leaving the Wall Street Journal and moving on to New Things
item.description: Joanna Stern is no stranger to new things. It’s part of the job: Stern began working as a technology journalist in 2007, the year Apple launched the first iPhone, and has covered the shifts in the industry through the rise of smartphones, the mobile internet, and AI. Along the way, she won an Emmy and...
item.source:markdown: Joanna Stern is no stranger to new things. It’s part of the job: Stern began working as a technology journalist in 2007, the year Apple launched the first iPhone, and has covered the shifts in the industry through the rise of smartphones, the mobile internet, and AI. Along the way, she won an Emmy and...

item.title: NPR may be flush with gifts to transform its tech, but it still has to cut jobs
item.description: Last month, NPR announced two private gifts totaling $113 million — among the largest donations it’s received in its history. The $80 million donation, from philanthropist Connie Ballmer, is specifically for “ensuring NPR transforms its technology to meet the needs and serve the interests of public media audiences on whatever platforms or devices they may...
item.source:markdown: Last month, NPR announced two private gifts totaling $113 million — among the largest donations it’s received in its history. The $80 million donation, from philanthropist Connie Ballmer, is specifically for “ensuring NPR transforms its technology to meet the needs and serve the interests of public media audiences on whatever platforms or devices they may...

item.title: Sam Altman backs “micropayment” model for AI agents to compensate publishers
item.description: Late last month, Sam Altman sat down with Nicholas Thompson, the CEO of The Atlantic, for a podcast episode of “The Most Interesting Thing in AI.” The show is produced by Re:think, the publication’s marketing and branded content studio. One clip has been making the rounds on social media the past couple days. In a...
item.source:markdown: Late last month, Sam Altman sat down with Nicholas Thompson, the CEO of The Atlantic, for a podcast episode of “The Most Interesting Thing in AI.” The show is produced by Re:think, the publication’s marketing and branded content studio. One clip has been making the rounds on social media the past couple days. In a...

item.title: Here’s a new database for local news research, from Syracuse University and Rebuild Local News
item.description: If you’re trying to get a handle on evidence from academic research about the state of local news, it’s hard to know where to start. The research is scattered — across disciplines from political science to economics to computer science; across universities; across paywalled journals. To some extent, it’s part of the academic job description...
item.source:markdown: If you’re trying to get a handle on evidence from academic research about the state of local news, it’s hard to know where to start. The research is scattered — across disciplines from political science to economics to computer science; across universities; across paywalled journals. To some extent, it’s part of the academic job description...

item.title: Creator journalism is the most disruptive shift the news industry has seen, ex-BBC News head says
item.description: If broadcasters want to rebuild trust and remain relevant, they must “liberate their talent” and let their journalists act more like independent creators, Deborah Turness said in a speech in London this week. “I believe the established media hasn’t confronted the hard truth that this revolution isn’t just about consumers moving to different platforms,” Turness,...
item.source:markdown: If broadcasters want to rebuild trust and remain relevant, they must “liberate their talent” and let their journalists act more like independent creators, Deborah Turness said in a speech in London this week. “I believe the established media hasn’t confronted the hard truth that this revolution isn’t just about consumers moving to different platforms,” Turness,...

item.title: Local stories by nonprofit college media resonated nationally at the start of 2026
item.description: At Nieman Lab, we’ve long covered the impact of student-run news outlets filling information gaps in their communities. Recent data from SimilarWeb shows just how far their work reaches. In February, three Southern college media outlets — The Duke Chronicle (Duke University), The Daily Tar Heel (University of North Carolina — Chapel Hill), and the...
item.source:markdown: At Nieman Lab, we’ve long covered the impact of student-run news outlets filling information gaps in their communities. Recent data from SimilarWeb shows just how far their work reaches. In February, three Southern college media outlets — The Duke Chronicle (Duke University), The Daily Tar Heel (University of North Carolina — Chapel Hill), and the...

item.title: When ICE came to Minneapolis, readers turned to The Minnesota Star Tribune’s free live blog
item.description: When Minneapolis became ground zero for the Trump administration’s unprecedented federal immigration crackdown, its local news institutions were, once again, on the front lines of a global story. And just as Minnesota’s nonprofits and public radio station punched above their audience weight with their reporting, so too did its local newspapers. The flagship Minnesota Star...
item.source:markdown: When Minneapolis became ground zero for the Trump administration’s unprecedented federal immigration crackdown, its local news institutions were, once again, on the front lines of a global story. And just as Minnesota’s nonprofits and public radio station punched above their audience weight with their reporting, so too did its local newspapers. The flagship Minnesota Star...

item.title: “Su legado sigue vivo”: Estudiantes de CSUN honran a Rodolfo Rudy Acuña

item.title: OPINION: My great-grandparents survived genocide before 1915
item.description: History marks 1915 as the beginning of the Armenian Genocide, but my ancestors knew its shadow long before the world gave it a date. My great-grandparents were originally settled in...

item.title: These for-profit local news sites have become the “papers” of record in their communities
item.description: In most American cities, the largest local newsroom still belongs to the local newspaper. But that certainly doesn’t mean the largest local news audience always goes to the people with a printing press. And in some cases, the local traffic champ isn’t even a TV station or public radio station — it’s a for-profit, digital-native...
item.source:markdown: In most American cities, the largest local newsroom still belongs to the local newspaper. But that certainly doesn’t mean the largest local news audience always goes to the people with a printing press. And in some cases, the local traffic champ isn’t even a TV station or public radio station — it’s a for-profit, digital-native...

item.title: Minnesota — and MPR — kept everyone’s attention at the start of 2026
item.description: Minnesota Public Radio’s coverage of ICE put it at the top of the traffic rankings for public media websites in the last quarter of 2025. That trend continued in the first quarter of 2026; according to our regular rankings derived from Similarweb data, MPR saw 7 million more visits in January than in December, driven in...
item.source:markdown: Minnesota Public Radio’s coverage of ICE put it at the top of the traffic rankings for public media websites in the last quarter of 2025. That trend continued in the first quarter of 2026; according to our regular rankings derived from Similarweb data, MPR saw 7 million more visits in January than in December, driven in...

item.title: #1 Istanbul Vita – The best hair transplant Clinic in 2026
item.description: Istanbul Vita Hair Transplant Clinic International patients consider Istanbul Vita to be one of the best hair transplant clinics in Turkey due to the fact that the clinic provides tailor-made...
item.source:markdown: Istanbul Vita Hair Transplant Clinic International patients consider Istanbul Vita to be one of the best hair transplant clinics in Turkey due to the fact that the clinic provides tailor-made...

item.title: “Affiliation, not just access”: Newsrooms try to move beyond membership to a focus on “belonging”
item.description: Facing declining trust, unreliable social platforms, and search traffic weakened by AI summaries, the media industry is looking for a deeper way to hold onto audiences. Could the answer be a sense of “belonging”? “I think belonging is trying to figure out ways for the audiences to feel heard and seen, to engage or learn...
item.source:markdown: Facing declining trust, unreliable social platforms, and search traffic weakened by AI summaries, the media industry is looking for a deeper way to hold onto audiences. Could the answer be a sense of “belonging”? “I think belonging is trying to figure out ways for the audiences to feel heard and seen, to engage or learn...

item.title: The European Union backs Italy’s right to make Meta pay for news
item.description: Meta must comply with Italian law requiring it to negotiate with and fairly compensate news publishers for the the use of their content, according to a ruling by the Europe’s highest court on Tuesday. The decision by the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) found that EU copyright law doesn’t prohibit individual countries...
item.source:markdown: Meta must comply with Italian law requiring it to negotiate with and fairly compensate news publishers for the the use of their content, according to a ruling by the Europe’s highest court on Tuesday. The decision by the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) found that EU copyright law doesn’t prohibit individual countries...

item.title: In Atlanta, the push for digital subscriptions hasn’t taken off as much as hoped
item.description: For American newspapers, the past decade-plus has been all about seeking digital subscribers. The print business is shot. Digital advertising is shot. And while not everyone can be The New York Times — now the proud provider of 13 million subscriptions — even most local and regional newspapers have made digital subs Priority No. 1....
item.source:markdown: For American newspapers, the past decade-plus has been all about seeking digital subscribers. The print business is shot. Digital advertising is shot. And while not everyone can be The New York Times — now the proud provider of 13 million subscriptions — even most local and regional newspapers have made digital subs Priority No. 1....

item.title: Behind the scenes at Wirecutter for an epic duel of air purifiers
item.description: Tim Heffernan lights five matches with a single stroke and waves them up and down to spread the smoke. He sets them on a dinner plate as the small conference room fills with the smell of burning wood. He checks his particle meter to make sure the room is sufficiently smoky, then steps around the...
item.source:markdown: Tim Heffernan lights five matches with a single stroke and waves them up and down to spread the smoke. He sets them on a dinner plate as the small conference room fills with the smell of burning wood. He checks his particle meter to make sure the room is sufficiently smoky, then steps around the...

item.title: Trolling, memes, and deepfakes: How AI is thickening the fog of war
item.description: War has never been fought only on the ground. Clausewitz’s concept of the “fog of war” once described the uncertainty and confusion that cloud battlefield decision-making. Errol Morris’ 2003 documentary made the phrase a shorthand for the moral and informational ambiguities of modern conflict. But in the digital age, where war is also filmed, edited and promoted...
item.source:markdown: War has never been fought only on the ground. Clausewitz’s concept of the “fog of war” once described the uncertainty and confusion that cloud battlefield decision-making. Errol Morris’ 2003 documentary made the phrase a shorthand for the moral and informational ambiguities of modern conflict. But in the digital age, where war is also filmed, edited and promoted...

item.title: ‘NELA’ offers a glimmer of hope with food on people’s tables
item.description: Just off of Figueroa Street in Highland Park sits The Offbeat. On any night from 5 p.m. to 2 a.m., music will be playing and drinks will be pouring. But...

item.title: César Chávez is being reexamined, where does that leave Chicano Studies?
item.description: On March 18, The New York Times published an investigative article alleging Cesar Chavez had sexually abused two adolescent girls consistently from 1972 to 1977. The two, along with fellow...
